title: Cacalia helophila
taxonomy: Cacalia helophila is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It belongs to the genus Cacalia, which is a small group of flowering plants. Cacalia helophila is native to East Asia, specifically in China, Japan, and Korea. The classification of Cacalia helophila is further supported by its morphological characteristics, such as its leaves and inflorescences. The species is often placed in the subfamily Carduoideae, which is a group of flowering plants that share similar characteristics with thistles and artichokes.
uses: Cacalia helophila is a plant species with limited information available, and as such, its medicinal uses are not well-documented. However, various sources suggest that it has been used in traditional medicine in Japan and other parts of Asia to treat various ailments. In traditional Japanese medicine, it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Some sources also suggest that it may have antimicrobial properties, which could help to combat infections. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Cacalia helophila for medicinal purposes.

preparations: Cacalia helophila is also known as swamp silvermoss or silver-leaved marshmallow.
